text: Polarized training intensity distribution improves VO2peak more than other training intensity distributions, with a small but statistically significant effect size.
population: trained endurance athletes (mixed elite and recreational)

text: Polarized training superiority for VO2peak improvement is specific to shorter interventions under 12 weeks, with a moderate effect size.
Polarized training superiority for VO2peak improvement is more pronounced in highly trained athletes compared to recreational athletes.
specific_value: SMD = 0.46 [95% CI 0.10, 0.82], p = 0.01 for highly trained

text: Polarized training does not improve time-trial performance more than other training intensity distributions.
population: trained endurance athletes (mixed elite and recreational)

text: Polarized training does not improve time to exhaustion more than other training intensity distributions.
population: trained endurance athletes (mixed elite and recreational)

text: Polarized training does not improve speed or power at the second ventilatory/lactate threshold more than other training intensity distributions.
population: trained endurance athletes (mixed elite and recreational)

text: Polarized training more effectively improves aerobic power (VO2peak) but is similar to other training intensity distributions for improving aerobic capacity (threshold-based measures and time-trial performance).
population: trained endurance athletes (mixed elite and recreational)
